reviews.review_msg: Bob is a great prof for this course. One of the easiest courses I've taken at SFU

Bob is a chill prof. It's ensc204, you draw a couple of things and you get an easy pass. idk how you fail the class but according to Bob a student did that twice lol. Legit the only way to fail is if you ghost the class or can't draw straight lines for your life.
not a bad guy explained everything you need for final exam. He'll be a five if he share his legos and that giant monitor with me

Mike is reasonable and usually teaches pretty easy but necessary stuff. If you go to lectures, you literally cannot fail. He post really helpful previous exams that help you learn and prep. Always take Mike.

well 204 was hard for me but mike was helpful during his office hours.  do all his assignment very carefully , don't copy from previous years. Midterm was ok and final was hard except the m/c questions  try to read the entire text book and all his notes carefully to get most of m/c right because the rest of final was pretty hard
That one credit course ruins my GPA. Even I have passed 251 and 252, but failed this 204!!
What bother teaching!? Go back to raw forest and ruin your position as a chair of industry
Bad grader i have ever seen and there is no concept that how the grade is given.
It is one credit course, but it feels like 400 level course with mike....Terrible, he will scale you about how hard the final is gonna be. Trust him on this.
Great Prof. Although if your not an engineering major, I wouldn't take this course. Seems easy but it can be frustrating for many people.

Very good prof. He is very good at conveying concepts that are quite abstract. Provides lots of examples. Assignments were short but somewhat difficult. Midterm and final were very fair. Marking was fair. He doesn't seem to be as bad as old reviews would suggest. I found it very easy to get an A+ in this course, but I do find this stuff fairly easy

I dont know why he chose teaching as his career because he clearly hates it! he doesnt care about the coarse load so he gives you lots of hard things to do, gives you extremly hard exams and then gives you a horrible mark at the end! "COOL DWN MIKE ITS JUST A ONE CREDIT COARSE"!

He puts you through huge course load and at the end give you an extremely unfair mark. Amount of the work required for this 1 credit course is more than most of 400 level courses you may take! I don't know what is he trying to prove by putting lots of pressure on students and giving them very low marks in his stupid 1 credit course!
Rating would have been 0 but, there's no option for that. Just wait until a different prof teaches this course. Good luck!

It's almost like he realizes it's an easy grade booster course and is arbitrarily making it harder to boost his own ego. Napoleon complex, arbitrary rules, unfair to students, the list goes on. People say you just gotta lock in and listen: I agree, if you paid attention the course itself is light, still would prefer another prof.
Love how they keep justice for Ajeet but delete the fact that Ajeet cheats and is getting called out for it.
Hold on a second let's not forget about the weirdos in my class talking about someone elses wife like that!

It was pretty rough at first, but Shervin owned up to his mistakes and honestly tried to fix things, and I respect that a lot. He really did his best to make sure students didn't feel confused, left out, or like their concerns weren't being heard. W prof!
We had a tough start with him, but he finally acknowledged it and owned up to it. He changed a lot of stuff to help us out at the end. He should be good for later classes
boycott ESSS everyone there is biased and bigoted anyways

I would not be shocked if Shervin brings this up next class and decides to play the victim card. The ESSS is corrupted and everyone there doesn't care. They only side with the faculty and staff because they want good references from professors and admins. None of them actually care about the students

No one's saying that Shervin isn't capable at teaching the content effectively; he's incredibly knowledgeable. The problem is his communication and the way in which he treats students. It's great to hear that students in his other courses don't have any issues, however that doesn't mean the rest are “exaggerating”.

It's funny how all the good reviews are coming from the ESSS council even though they never had this teacher and they just licking ahh. SFU is a disappointment, all they did was hold the students accountable and made the teacher look like the victim. Don't believe the good reviews, sfu wiped over a 100 bad reviews.
If one person thinks the prof isn't professional than it's probably just on them but when majority of the students in a course have issues than it's absolutely on the prof to rectify their behaviour
worst teacher ever. I never write reviews but this professor made my life miserable
he traumatized me for life. DO NOT take this class.

has no respect for students and only became lenient after countless emails from students to the engineering department he often tries to scare students by falsely accusing them of receiving the lowest scores to make a point that his exam will be “difficult”

Shervin is often unreasonable. He includes a 5% “bonus” component, but the questions are so difficult that almost no one gets them right, and even correct answers earn as little as 0.5%. Many students end up guessing, since his strict grading on exams and assignments has many already failing or close to it.

Shervin gives overly difficult in-class assignments that leave many students confused, yet he still grades them for accuracy. It would be fairer to grade for completion if attendance is the goal. He also spends too long on pop quizzes and surprise tasks, causing him to miss planned lecture material.

Shervin seemed to use his authority unfairly, especially with project idea presentations. Only after higher staff stepped in did he change anything. Students had waited hours before, some never presenting, and he'd threaten zeros for missing members. He never apologized or explained—he simply adjusted once his position was at risk.
Super smazing profieesor good

One of the best professors I've had at SFU Engineering. Shervin is reliable, knowledgeable, and genuinely cares about students. The midterm was easy with an 85% average. Labs were simple if you followed the textbook, and in-class assignments were straightforward. Truly the best professor. He should make the class harder! It is too easy!
Horrible organization and no respect for students time
strict prof very aggressive doesnt care for student well beings dont take at all rather grad 2 years late than take with shervin
THE WORST PROF EVER. SO UNPROFFERINALY UNORGANZIED. AVOID THIS COURSE. ITS NOT EVEN A PRE REQ FOR ANYTHING. LITERALLY LEARN SLIDWORKS ON YOUR OWN.

I'll give him a double zero. Expect to waste tens of hours through the class because of his inclear instructions. Very unorganized. Didn't give the TAs the answer sheet for the midterm so the TAs had to make their own. Worst class experience I had at SFU for sure.
Very tough marking and strict.
Avoid taking a class with him if u can

Prof will take attendance if lecture numbers are low and gives out bonus marks to in class participation. Really clear lectures and easy to understand assignments. Exams are all based on the worksheets and assignments given. Quizzes were open book on canvas based on lectures. Prof is really responsive and easy to approach. nice chill guy
